task_name: Find and summarize merged PRs by contributor

tool_input.command: gh pr list --repo anthropics/anthropic-sdk-python --author karpetrosyan --state merged --json number,title,body --limit 100
gh search prs --repo anthropics/anthropic-sdk-python --author karpetrosyan --state merged --json number,title,body --limit 100

tool_calls.tool_output:
STDERR: invalid argument "merged" for "--state" flag: valid values are {open|closed}

Usage:  gh search prs [<query>] [flags]

Flags:
      --app string              Filter by GitHub App author
      --archived                Filter based on the repository archived state {true|false}
      --assignee string         Filter by assignee
      --author string           Filter by author
  -B, --base string             Filter on base branch name
      --checks string           Filter based on status of the checks: {pending|success|failure}
      --closed date             Filter on closed at date
      --commenter user          Filter based on comments by user
      --comments number         Filter on number of comments
      --created date            Filter based on created at date
      --draft                   Filter based on draft state
  -H, --head string             Filter on head branch name
      --interactions number     Filter on number of reactions and comments
      --involves user           Filter based on involvement of user
  -q, --jq expression           Filter JSON output using a jq expression
      --json fields             Output JSON with the specified fields
      --label strings           Filter on label
      --language string         Filter based on the coding language
  -L, --limit int               Maximum number of results to fetch (default 30)
      --locked                  Filter on locked conversation status
      --match strings           Restrict search to specific field of issue: {title|body|comments}
      --mentions user           Filter based on user mentions
      --merged                  Filter based on merged state
      --merged-at date          Filter on merged at date
      --milestone title         Filter by milestone title
      --no-assignee             Filter on missing assignee
      --no-label                Filter on missing label
      --no-milestone            Filter on missing milestone
      --no-project              Filter on missing project
      --order string            Order of results returned, ignored unless '--sort' flag is specified: {asc|desc} (default "desc")
      --owner strings           Filter on repository owner
      --project owner/number    Filter on project board owner/number
      --reactions number        Filter on number of reactions
  -R, --repo strings            Filter on repository
      --review string           Filter based on review status: {none|required|approved|changes_requested}
      --review-requested user   Filter on user or team requested to review
      --reviewed-by user        Filter on user who reviewed
      --sort string             Sort fetched results: {comments|reactions|reactions-+1|reactions--1|reactions-smile|reactions-thinking_face|reactions-heart|reactions-tada|interactions|created|updated} (default "best-match")
      --state string            Filter based on state: {open|closed}
      --team-mentions string    Filter based on team mentions
  -t, --template string         Format JSON output using a Go template; see "gh help formatting"
      --updated date            Filter on last updated at date
      --visibility strings      Filter based on repository visibility: {public|private|internal}
  -w, --web                     Open the search query in the web browser

tool_input.command: gh search prs --repo anthropics/anthropic-sdk-python --author karpetrosyan --merged --json number,title,body --limit 100

[{"body":"This reverts commit 062077e50d182719637403576f59761999b3b2f5.","number":1158,"title":"fix(structured outputs): send structured output beta header when format is omitted"},{"body":"Third-party clients (Vertex, Bedrock) don’t support structured outputs and its beta header, so when the SDK sets it, the remote server returns `Error code: 400 - {'message': 'invalid beta flag'}.`

This change just avoids always setting that header, so we can still port the `tool_runner` and `stream` helpers, which use `parse` method internally, with minimal changes.","number":1121,"title":"fix(structured outputs): avoid including beta header if `output_format` is missing"},{"body":"Before:

```python
    @pytest.mark.respx(base_url=base_url)
    def test_basic_call_sync(self, client: Anthropic, respx_mock: MockRouter, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
        @beta_tool
        def get_weather(location: str, units: Literal["c", "f"]) -> BetaFunctionToolResultType:
            """Lookup the weather for a given city in either celsius or fahrenheit

            Args:
                location: The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA
                units: Unit for the output, either 'c' for celsius or 'f' for fahrenheit
            Returns:
                A dictionary containing the location, temperature, and weather condition.
            """
            return json.dumps(_get_weather(location, units))

        message = make_snapshot_request(
            lambda c: c.beta.messages.tool_runner(
                max_tokens=1024,
                model="claude-haiku-4-5",
                tools=[get_weather],
                messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is the weather in SF?"}],
            ).until_done(),
            content_snapshot=snapshots["basic"]["responses"],
            path="/v1/messages",
            mock_client=client,
            respx_mock=respx_mock,
        )
```

def create_summary(title, body):
    """Create a one-sentence summary based on title and body"""
    if "fix(structured outputs): send structured output beta header when format is omitted" in title:
        return "Reverts a previous commit to fix structured output beta header handling when format is omitted."
    elif "fix(structured outputs): avoid including beta header if `output_format` is missing" in title:
        return "Prevents sending structured output beta headers to third-party clients that don't support them to avoid 400 errors."
    elif "chore(internal): simplify http snapshots" in title:
        return "Simplifies HTTP snapshot testing by removing complex mock setup and using a cleaner parametrized approach."
    elif "feat: allow raw JSON schema to be passed to `messages.stream()`" in title:
        return "Adds support for passing raw JSON schemas directly to the messages.stream() method."
    elif "feat(tool runner): add support for server-side tools" in title:
        return "Adds functionality to support server-side tools in the tool runner feature."
    elif "chore(internal): remove unnecessary wrapper around external snapshots" in title:
        return "Removes redundant wrapper code around external snapshots that was breaking inline snapshot fixes."
    elif "fix(client): resolve non-functional default socket options" in title:
        return "Fixes issues with default socket options that were not functioning properly in the client."
    elif "docs: explain snapshot update process" in title:
        return "Adds documentation explaining how to update snapshots in the testing process."
    elif "fix(tests): suppress warnings in tests when running on the latest Python versions" in title:
        return "Suppresses deprecation warnings from datetime.utcnow() when running tests on newer Python versions."
    elif "feat(bedrock): automatically infer AWS Region in Bedrock clients" in title:
        return "Adds automatic AWS region inference functionality for Bedrock clients."
    elif "fix(httpx): resolve conflict between default transport and proxy settings" in title:
        return "Resolves conflicts that occur between default transport settings and proxy configurations in httpx."
    else:
        return "Updates functionality based on the PR title and description."

result = []
for pr in data:
    result.append({
        "number": pr["number"],
        "title": pr["title"],
        "summary": create_summary(pr["title"], pr["body"])
    })

# Sort by PR number descending
result.sort(key=lambda x: x["number"], reverse=True)

print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
EOF
